text: The lesion was 8 cm from the anal verge on the left lateral wall, which gave us adequate room for deployment of the robotic SP arms in the rectal vault.
For this reason, we chose the Tammis GelPoint Path device for access.
This device was inserted transanally and secured to the perianal tissues with 2-0 proline sutures.
The SP port and an additional 5mm air seal trocar was placed through the gel point path.
Pneumo insufflation was initiated using the air seal device.
The SP robotic port was then docked.
Using the monopolar scissors, a one centimeter margin was scored around the area of the lesion and a full thickness excision was carried out.
To facilitate the dissection, the hemostatic clip that was placed at the time of colonoscopy was used for retraction.
The non-fragmented specimen was then placed to the side in the rectal vault.
The defect in the rectal wall was repaired using a running 2-0 PDS V-lock suture.
The rectum and anal canal were examined carefully to ensure adequate hemostasis and to ensure there was no compromise of the rectal lumen.
All instruments were removed and the robot was undocked.
The specimen was extracted and the gel point path was removed.
Due to the proximity of the lesion to the anal rectal ring, we decided to use the intuitive bubble access port.
This port allows for better arm deployment in the chamber as compared to the traditional gel point path device.
The intuitive bubble access port was inserted trans-anally and secured to the perianal tissues using a true O proline suture.
In order to enhance visualization and facilitate the resection, the camera was rotated 180 degrees.
A one centimeter margin was marked using monopolar cautery.
The incision was extended through the mucosa, submucosa, and muscularis propria layers.
Using the monopolar scissors, the lesion was excised, taking full thickness rectal wall as well as the mesorectal fat.
Hemostasis was obtained as needed using bipolar cautery.
The specimen was excised in one piece and placed in the bubble chamber for removal.
The defect in the rectal wall was repaired using a running V-lock 2-0 PDS suture.
The specimen was extracted and the access port was removed.
